steps.title: Set off the interruption
steps.content: The phrase beginning especially since interrupts the core statement she's best known as a novelist. Because the sentence already closes that interruption with a dash before as a novelist, the blank needs the matching opening dash.

steps.title: Match the existing dash
steps.content: Choice A leaves the interruption unmarked, Choice B uses a colon where an aside is needed, and Choice D uses a semicolon before a dependent phrase. The dash opens the aside cleanly. The answer is C.
